arXiv.title: Muon magnetic anomaly: experimental status and prospects
arXiv.comments: Paper submitted for publication in the proceedings of PASCOS 2024: the 29th International Symposium on Particles, String and Cosmology
arXiv.abstract: The past five years have brought significant new results on the muon magnetic anomaly, $a_Î¼= (g_Î¼- 2)/2$, and on the hadronic vacuum polarization (HVP) contribution dominating the uncertainty $Îa_Î¼$. Serious tension has emerged between the experimental and standard model(SM) values for $a_Î¼$, as well as between the SM and the first precise lattice QCD values. We review the current experimental and theoretical status of $a_Î¼$, along with the prospects for new results,focusing on MUonE,a new experiment at CERN, aiming to evaluate the leading order contribution to a(HVP) in a direct measurement of muonic Bhabha scattering.
